summary: postinstall exfiltrates env,.npmrc tokens, and host filesystem to hardcoded bare-IP C2
details: On npm install, postinstall.js executes a shell pipeline that enumerates installer identity (`id`, `hostname`, `pwd`, `date`, `env | sort`), reads candidate npm credential files (`/app/.npmrc`, `/usr/etc/npmrc`, `/root/.npmrc`), dumps `/app` and `/` listings, project files (`Cargo.toml`, `package.json`, `package-lock.json`), `/proc/*/cmdline`, and greps for HTB-style flag and secret strings. Output is base64-encoded, chunked, and PUT to `http://154.57.164.71:30782` over plain HTTP via `http.request({hostname, port, path, method:'PUT'})`. Execution is gated by `isChallengeWorker()`, which requires cwd to include `/app/node_modules` and hostname to match `/^[0-9a-f]{12}$/` (Docker-style 12-hex), evading casual sandbox analysis while firing in CTF/CI containers. The.npmrc reads are direct credential theft — any tokens present allow the attacker to publish packages as the installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
